variables.name: AI productivity offset (magnitude × timing)
state_space: concentrated monopoly-rent gains, no CPI diffusion

actor_readings.reading: Frontier position, FCF-funded hyperscalers (#33); best-placed for offset but also highest stranded-asset exposure. Medium confidence.
Indigenizing at higher cost under export controls (#39); offset diluted by duplicative stack spend and demographic demand deficit (#58).
Lagging on frontier compute and energy cost; least likely to capture an endogenous offset. Low-confidence positive.

variables.current_reading: Sits in the 'lag / uncertain' band with medium confidence. Unit costs falling in AI-exposed sectors (#4) but economy-wide TFP not yet visible; capex ($500B+ run-rate, #3) is contemporaneous while payoff is historically 5–15yr out (#14). Crowding-out (#24) and monopoly-rent capture (#31) make net additionality unproven.
variables.trajectory: Direction positive but slow-clock and hard to catch up on the diffusion leg: internet TFP took ~10yr to show (#A19), electrification 5–15yr (#A20). The compute-cost/energy feedback (#99) could accelerate it; grid/minerals (#A-none, #98) decelerate it. Catch-up-able for followers on models, NOT on the physical diffusion timeline.
variables.base_rate: Prior GP-tech waves (telecom #A19, electrification #A20, Second Industrial Revolution #A22) delivered real but lagged TFP; offset materialized in ~1 of 2 cases within a decade. Breaks because today's fiscal starting point is a 6–7% structural deficit, not the Clinton surplus (#A19), so the 'grow out of it' math is far harder.

variables.name: Central-bank independence vs fiscal-dominance regime

actor_readings.reading: Faces VISIBLE open-market discipline; cannot repress without shattering reserve status (#37). Plumbing (SRF/SLR) may force dominance by market necessity, not political capture (#73 #75). Medium-high probability of soft repression by ~2030.
Resolves QUIETLY — closed capital account permits administered rates and directed credit without flight (#37, #A11, #A14). Effectively already in a managed regime; binding constraint lies elsewhere.
Already through one YCC cycle and disorderly 2024 unwind (#15); dual-anchor war-finance stress (#90) is the live risk. Least institutional runway.
Austerity attempted 2010–15 (#110) burned that path; ECB independence strong (Bundesbank legacy #A32) but fiscal fragmentation limits repression tools.

variables.current_reading: Trending toward soft repression with medium confidence but not yet realized: no G7 CB formally subordinated (#11), but debt service ~20% of revenue, SRF/SLR make operational dominance latent (#73 #75). Inflation-expectations de-anchoring could make it self-fulfilling before formal capture (#20).
variables.trajectory: Slow political-economy drift punctuated by non-linear plumbing triggers. Democracies show a revealed preference to inflate-away (#106); duration cliff (#16) and entitlements (#59) compress the clock into 2025–2030. Very hard to reverse once inertial inflation is institutionalized (#A15 Brazil) — reversal requires a crisis-forced Simsek/Volcker shock (#A36, #A31).
variables.base_rate: WWII US ran YCC + 15–20% inflation to cut real debt/GDP ~40pp (#A1, #9); repression is historically survivable but required a discrete institutional rupture (Accord 1951) to exit. Breaks: 1940s had closed capital account; open capital mobility today makes yield caps far harder to sustain.

variables.name: Neutral real rate (r*) / global loanable-funds balance

actor_readings.reading: 10y real yields ~1–2%; deficit issuance is the marginal demand shock. Medium confidence r* stays elevated.
Domestic r* suppressed administratively; excess savings + demographic overcapacity keep internal rates low (#58, #A11).

variables.current_reading: Moderate elevation with medium confidence. Twin AI+energy capex (#3 #13) plus rearmament (#79) and duplicative tech stacks (#39) raise demand for capital; demographic dissaving (#57) raises it further. Offsets: industrial policy may crowd IN private capital (#28) and pension decumulation adds a duration bid (#65).
variables.trajectory: Upward drift, medium-clock, structurally sticky if demographic dissaving dominates (#57) — that leg is not catch-up-able. Fiscal-multiplier compression at high debt (#26) and China deflation export (#36) are the counter-currents that could cap it.
variables.base_rate: Post-2010 savings glut pushed r* deeply negative; reversal analogous to no clean prior case — closest is the loanable-funds competition of Victorian railway mania layered on war debt (#A4). Breaks: today's issuer is world's largest net debtor, so higher r* burdens the sovereign rather than rewarding it.

variables.name: Binding physical input (power, grid, minerals, capital goods, leading-edge chips)

actor_readings.reading: Grid/power and transmission are the binding inputs (#8 #98); land-near-power and HV engineers scarce. High confidence this gates US buildout.
Leading-edge logic chips are THE binding input under export controls (#18 #39); but grid/power and minerals are relative strengths (dominant solar/refining).
Energy cost + capital-goods competition for the same fabs/grid kit (#45); binding on competitiveness.
Power-abundant (cheap gas + solar), so physical inputs are a comparative advantage, not a constraint — hence attractive for sovereign-AI compute siting (#21).

variables.current_reading: Structural bottleneck regime, high confidence. Grid interconnect queues, transformer/copper/specialized-labor scarcity (#8 #13), critical-mineral mine lead-times 10–15yr (#30), transmission upgrades 15–20yr (#98). Synchronized global industrial policy removed the off-peak buffer (#45).
variables.trajectory: Slow to clear on the hardest legs: mines and transmission cannot be built on political timelines (#30 #98) — a decade-plus floor. Chips are faster to catch up on than grid/minerals. Wright's-Law learning curves (#94) attack the cost side but not the permitting/physical-lead-time side.
variables.base_rate: Every prior capex supercycle hit a real-resource wall that inflation paid for (Korea HCI #A8, China post-GFC #A11). Breaks: those built long-life hard assets; AI capex is 5–8yr-life and obsolescence-prone, so the bottleneck bites before assets amortize.

variables.name: Energy-cost trajectory (abundance vs J-curve)
state_space: grid bottleneck severs generation from delivery

actor_readings.reading: Dominant solar manufacturing + grid buildout; closest to in-window abundance, reinforcing deflation export (#36 #41).
Generation cheap but grid/permitting bottleneck (#98) delays delivered deflation past the fiscal window. J-curve risk high.
Structural solar-irradiance advantage (#100) reshapes into a new energy-geopolitical axis.

variables.current_reading: Split with medium confidence: solar+storage LCOS already sub-$50/MWh in prime sites (#91 #94), but SMRs 10–15yr out (#97) and transmission severs cheap generation from delivered cost on the fiscal-relevant timeline (#98). Net near-term effect likely inflationary (J-curve #97) before deflationary.
variables.trajectory: Two clocks in tension: generation cost falling fast (Wright's Law, #94), delivery infrastructure fixed at 15–20yr (#98). The crux is whether deflation reaches industrial users before fiscal dominance locks in (#91). Fossil stranded-asset credit event (#95) and fiscal revenue loss from energy deflation (#96 #101) are downside kickers.
variables.base_rate: Shale (#A23) and shale-gas showed ZIRP-funded energy capex producing durable deflationary supply — but with sector-specific busts. Breaks: energy-abundance thesis assumes generation cost = delivered cost, which the grid bottleneck falsifies on the relevant horizon.

variables.name: Dollar reserve status / marginal UST demand ('who buys the bonds')

actor_readings.reading: Reserve issuer; deficits both supply global liquidity AND erode confidence (Triffin, #22 #A2 #A13). Binding exposure — cannot repress without losing the bid. Medium-high confidence slow erosion, tail risk of rupture.
Holds ~$750B–$1T UST as a binary weapon/target (#82); building CIPS/mBridge/gold hedge (#A30 #51). Chokepoint actor on the demand side.
Petrodollar recycling in structural decline (#29 #A28); mBridge participant (#50). Marginal-buyer defection risk.

variables.current_reading: Slow-erosion-with-strong-floor, medium-high confidence. Dollar share ~57–58% and strengthened post-2022 (#A25); sovereign UST bid shrinking (weaponization #7, petrodollar decline #29, #A28) but offset by stablecoins (#47 #48), spontaneous EM dollarization (#49), and tokenized UST (#56). Triffin contradiction live (#22).
variables.trajectory: Gradual until sudden — the recurring pattern (#A13 #A25 #A27 #A29). Weaponization and debasement are complementary accelerants raising the marginal cost of holding dollars (#A25). Payment-rail chokepoint (mBridge #50) is the operational fork. Erosion is catch-up-able only via alternative rails, which are being built (#A30) but blocked by RMB capital controls.
variables.base_rate: Sterling took ~30yr to lose reserve status and needed explicit guarantees + IMF backstop to do so orderly (#A29); Amsterdam collapsed in ~5yr after 40yr of hidden erosion (#A27). Breaks: dollar's network depth dwarfs any prior incumbent and there is no convertible alternative at scale (RMB capital-controlled, #A30).

variables.name: Treasury-market plumbing / non-linear stress capacity
state_space: flash-crash forcing permanent backstop (operational fiscal dominance)

actor_readings.reading: SRF + SLR make refusal-to-monetize option effectively gone (#75); this is the operational channel by which US fiscal dominance arrives regardless of politics. High leverage on V2.
JGB + UST dual-anchor stress under war finance (#90); BOJ normalization competes with fiscal need.

variables.current_reading: Fragile, medium-high confidence. SLR/GSIB constraints cap dealer warehousing exactly as issuance grows (#73 #77); ON-RRP buffer drained (#76); basis trade $600B–$1T+ (#74) and stablecoin run risk (#52) are live fault lines. SRF is a standing liquidity put (#75).
variables.trajectory: Sudden-onset risk, hours-not-days (#52 #74). Each stress event ratchets the implicit backstop larger (#78). Rules get suspended in extremis — structural feature since Peel's Act (#A17). Not catch-up-able; it is a standing vulnerability that only worsens with issuance volume.
variables.base_rate: March 2020, 2022 UK LDI (#27), April 2025 basis unwind (#74) all forced emergency intervention mid-tightening. Consistent pattern: plumbing beats policy stance. Breaks little — this is a robust, repeatedly-observed mechanism.

variables.name: Geopolitical discontinuity (Taiwan/TSMC + rearmament supercycle)

actor_readings.reading: Controls the TSMC-proximate escalation lever AND holds the UST weapon (#82); chokepoint actor. Also builds strategic stockpiles as price-inelastic demand floor (#40).
Faces simultaneous war-finance expansion + chip supply shock = stagflationary worst case (#83); would resort to capital controls, collapsing open-market discipline (#81).
Treaty-ally; a Taiwan contingency forces immediate defense spend competing with JGB stability (#90).

variables.current_reading: Peacetime-fragmentation-premium now, with a fat conflict tail, medium confidence. Reshoring is a one-way inflation ratchet (#10); tension-insurance premium is a continuous floor (#89); rearmament is a co-equal fiscal supercycle with no productivity offset (#79). TSMC/Taiwan is a binary crux on whether the capex cycle can even complete (#18).
variables.trajectory: The premium leg is a slow ratchet; the kinetic leg is a step-discontinuity that would resolve V2 by emergency fiat (#80), detonate V6 (freeze/dump #82 #85), and impose a 3–5yr semiconductor shock (#83). Defense-AI spillover could paradoxically strengthen the productivity offset (#87). Not catch-up-able — a single event dominates all clocks.
variables.base_rate: Hot war historically forces sub-inflation rate suppression within weeks, not years, and capital controls (#80 #81, #A1 WWII). Enemy-asset sequestration precedent set at ~$300B (Russia); China scale is ~10x (#82 #85). Breaks: no precedent for weaponizing reserves against a peer-sized holder — partially self-deterring (#A25).

variables.name: Inflation transmission / distributional channel

actor_readings.reading: Inflationary bias in services/energy/labor; goods disinflation imported. Mortgage lock-in (#103) and housing-equity 'third mandate' (#104) blunt transmission and constrain the CB politically.
Structurally deflationary from demographic demand deficit + overcapacity (#58 #36); exports deflation to import-exposed West (#41).
Import-exposed to China deflation while facing reshoring/energy cost floors — squeezed both ways.

variables.current_reading: Bifurcated regime most likely, medium confidence: AI rents concentrate in equity without CPI diffusion (#31), goods deflate via China overcapacity (#36) while services stay sticky on demographic demand (#62). Headline CPI can read 'on target' while mis-signaling policy (#31 #62).
variables.trajectory: The bifurcation widens as aging shifts demand to AI-resistant services (#62) and China's structural (not strategic) deflation export persists for a generation (#58). Displacement-vs-demographic-contraction race differs by decade — demographic contraction faster in 2020s, AI faster in 2030s (#63). Hard to re-anchor once CPI mis-measures.
variables.base_rate: China post-GFC showed capex without income redistribution → deflation (#A11); platform consolidation 2010s showed rents in equity not CPI (#31). Breaks: US labor markets less indexed and MPC dynamics differ from creditor-China.

variables.name: Political resolution-path selection (who bears the adjustment)

actor_readings.reading: Coalition equilibrium: homeowners block rate hikes (#104), equity holders block wealth taxes (#107), retirees block repression, wage earners resist inflation → inflate-away is the residual. Medium confidence.
Austerity path already exhausted 2010–15 (#110); less runway, pushed toward repression.
Administers the adjustment invisibly via directed credit and repressed deposit rates (#37, #A14); no electoral coalition constraint.

variables.current_reading: Tilted toward inflate-away/repression, medium confidence. Democracies reveal a preference for the diffuse inflation tax over traceable austerity (#106); aging median voter blocks entitlement reform (#60); equity top-quintile blocks wealth taxes (#107); AI-accelerated avoidance erodes the tax-base path (#109). Order of attempts determines the forced endpoint (#110).
variables.trajectory: Path-dependent and sequential — each attempted path burns the coalition for the next (#110). Constituency lock-in makes industrial-policy spending self-perpetuating (#112). Rising renter share is a slow tipping point that could re-enable housing repricing / normalization over 10–20yr (#113). Slow political clock, but the duration cliff (#16) can force it abruptly.
variables.base_rate: Greenback era (#A6) and interwar Europe show hard-money-wins is possible but brutal and coalition-specific; democratic polities have historically chosen inflation (#106, #A15). Breaks: US has no external anchor to 'borrow credibility' from (#A35 Italy/EMU) — resolution must be self-generated.

edges.note: Productivity offset cannot diffuse without power/grid/minerals/chips; transmission 15–20yr lead severs cheap compute from delivered TFP (#13 #98 #30).
Energy abundance relieves the power-input bottleneck, but only if delivered before the grid bottleneck bites (#99 #91 #98).
Energy is 50–70% of AI compute cost; a 5–10x electricity price fall drives a nonlinear compute-cost/productivity feedback (#99).
If the offset arrives contemporaneously, deficits self-liquidate and fiscal dominance is avoided; if lagged, CBs face the holding problem and drift toward repression (#1 #14).
Leading-edge chips concentrated in TSMC/Taiwan; the binding input IS the geopolitical flashpoint, controlled by the China-Taiwan axis (#18 #39).
A Taiwan contingency terminates hyperscaler capex and imposes a 3–5yr semiconductor supply shock, aborting the offset (#18 #83).
China's ~$750B–$1T UST can be frozen (US) or dumped (China) as a binary demand-destruction event (#82 #85 #86).
Hot conflict forces war-speed monetary financing + capital controls within weeks, resolving the dominance crux by emergency fiat (#80 #81, #A1).
For the open-capital-account US, loss of the marginal UST bid forces either higher yields or Fed absorption — the channel by which fiscal dominance arrives (#7 #22 #37).
SLR/dealer limits + SRF standing put convert Treasury-supply overflow into forced Fed intervention regardless of policy stance — operational dominance before political capture (#73 #75).
Higher r* / savings scarcity raises the yield the US must offer to attract the marginal buyer, tightening the who-buys-the-bonds bind (#3 #23 #57).
Rising rates trigger LDI/basis/duration stress that forces backstops (#27 #74), linking neutral-rate elevation to plumbing fragility.
Shrinking sovereign bid + stablecoin/tokenized-UST fragility means demand shocks propagate into fire-sale plumbing events at machine speed (#52 #56 #76).
Bifurcated/monopoly-rent inflation keeps headline CPI on-target, removing the CB mandate to tighten and enabling continued fiscal expansion (#31 #62).
Whether AI gains diffuse broadly or concentrate as equity rents determines the inflation transmission channel and MPC (#12 #31 #32).
The distributional incidence of inflation (services vs goods, capital vs labor) shapes which constituency can be taxed and thus which resolution path is politically feasible (#107 #104).
The chosen political path (inflate-away vs repression vs austerity vs wealth tax) selects which V2 regime materializes; democracies bias toward inflation (#106 #110).
US-China race + duplicative tech stacks + rearmament add symmetric bloc capex, pushing world r* above any single-bloc projection (#35 #39 #79).
Energy deflation hollows fuel-excise/carbon/fossil-corporate tax bases exactly as debt-service peaks, worsening the fiscal gap and constraining the tax path (#96 #101).
Actor-split: China can repress rates without capital flight (closed account) so V2 does NOT threaten its bond demand; the US repressing would shatter reserve status (#37, #A30).

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will US nonfarm labor-productivity growth (BLS, trailing 8-quarter annualized) exceed 2.5% at any print through end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: BLS official productivity release showing trailing-8Q annualized nonfarm output/hour >2.5% in any quarter up to Q4-2027. Single-source: BLS.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the Federal Reserve restart net balance-sheet expansion (outright UST purchases, excluding scheduled reinvestment and emergency repo) before end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Fed H.4.1 shows a sustained (>3-month) rise in outright UST holdings attributable to a new purchase program, by 31 Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will foreign official holdings fall below 30% of marketable US Treasury debt outstanding by end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: US Treasury TIC + SIFMA data show foreign official UST holdings / total marketable outstanding <30% at any month-end by Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a Taiwan Strait kinetic blockade or conflict materially disrupting TSMC output occur before end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Reported PLA blockade/kinetic action causing TSMC to announce a fab-output halt or force-majeure, confirmed by two major wire services, by 31 Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a US grid operator (PJM/ERCOT/MISO) report data-center interconnection or new-load queue waits exceeding 5 years during 2026?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: An official ISO/RTO or FERC filing in 2026 stating typical large-load interconnection timelines >5 years.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the Fed conduct an unscheduled Treasury-market stabilization intervention (outside routine ops) before end-2026?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Fed announces an emergency asset purchase, SRF activation at scale, or ad-hoc market-functioning purchase before 31 Dec 2026, reported by the Fed and major press.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will Lazard's LCOE report show US utility-scale solar-plus-storage below $40/MWh (unsubsidized midpoint) by its 2027 edition?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Lazard LCOE+ (or successor) 2027 report lists utility-scale solar+storage unsubsidized midpoint <$40/MWh.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the US 10-year TIPS real yield average above 1.5% over calendar-year 2026?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Treasury/FRED daily 10Y TIPS yield, simple 2026 calendar-year average >1.5%.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the PBoC formally adopt an explicit long-rate cap or announce a large directed-credit / bond-purchase program by end-2028?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: PBoC official statement of a long-yield target/cap or a named large-scale bond-buying/directed-credit program, by 31 Dec 2028. Measures China's administered-repression path.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will total stablecoin-held US Treasuries exceed $500B by end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Aggregated issuer attestations (USDT, USDC, et al.) or a Treasury/industry tracker show combined UST reserves >$500B at any month-end by Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a US federal wealth tax, mark-to-market billionaire tax, or hyperscaler excess-profits levy be enacted by end-2028?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: US federal statute enacting a net-wealth, mark-to-market unrealized-gains, or targeted excess-profits tax signed into law by 31 Dec 2028.

binding_constraint_by_actor.reading: Minimum stave is the demand-and-plumbing pair V6→V7: as the open-capital-account issuer it cannot repress without losing the marginal UST bid, and SLR/SRF plumbing (#73 #75) converts any supply overflow into forced Fed absorption. Fiscal dominance therefore arrives by market necessity, not political choice — gated further by whether V1's productivity offset clears before the duration cliff (#16). Medium-high confidence.

Minimum stave is V4 physical inputs — specifically leading-edge logic chips under export controls (#18 #39). Its fiscal/monetary endgame is NOT binding because the closed capital account lets it repress rates and direct credit invisibly (#37, #A14). Its constraint is technological, not financial; it clears only via indigenization on a slow, expensive chip clock.

Minimum stave is V5/V4 — energy cost and capital-goods/fab competition (#45) — compounded by fiscal fragmentation (no fiscal union to run V2 repression). Squeezed between imported China goods-deflation and domestic reshoring/energy cost floors (#41). Austerity path already exhausted (#110).

Minimum stave is V6/V5 — the petrodollar-recycling decision as oil revenue structurally declines (#29 #A28) and mBridge offers a non-dollar rail (#50). Energy is a comparative advantage, so the binding choice is reserve-allocation and settlement-rail alignment, which in aggregate helps set the pace of dollar erosion.

Minimum stave is V7/V2 — JGB-market stability under demographic dissaving and the unfinished YCC unwind (#15 #64), with dual-anchor JGB+UST stress the acute tail if a Taiwan contingency forces defense spending (#90). Least institutional runway of the anchors.

summary: The graph is a two-layer machine: a real-economy layer (can AI+energy productivity clear before physical bottlenecks and debt-rollover force a regime change?) sitting on top of a monetary-plumbing layer (who buys the bonds, and does the Fed retain the option to refuse?). The master gate is V1 (productivity offset) with a 5–15yr clock (#14, #A19, #A20), but V1 is itself gated by physical bottlenecks V4 (power/grid/minerals/chips, #13 #30 #98) and relieved by V5 (energy abundance, #99). Whether the offset arrives in time is a race against the duration cliff (#16) and entitlement lock-in (#59). Crucially the endgame is NOT one scenario: capital-account asymmetry (#37, #A30) bifurcates it — the US faces open-market discipline where V6 (UST demand) and V7 (dealer/plumbing) are the binding staves that can convert fiscal dominance into a fait accompli via market necessity (#73 #75), while China resolves the same pressure quietly through administered rates and directed credit, so its binding stave is instead physical (leading-edge compute, #39). The dominant mutual chokepoint is Taiwan/TSMC (V8): a hot contingency simultaneously terminates the capex cycle, triggers war-finance repression, and detonates the UST-holdings question (#18 #82 #83), collapsing five other variables at once. Honest read: contested and clock-dependent — the West's inflationary path and East Asia's demographic-deflationary path (#41 #58 #61) may converge only late, and the outcome hinges on relative clearing speeds of V1/V5 versus V6/V7, not on any single actor "winning."
